headline: Malaysia Ranks 32nd in Global Innovation Index Rising 5 Spots
context: WIPO Global Innovation Index 2025 ranked Malaysia 32nd up 5 places from 2024 driven by patent filings and R&D spending.

cards.big: Malaysia rises to 32nd in Global Innovation Index up 5 places
cards.sub: Patent filings increased 28% to 12,800 in 2025 highest ever recorded

cards.big: R&D spending reached 1.4% of GDP up from 1.1% in 2022
cards.sub: Private sector R&D contribution grew from 52% to 65% of total

cards.big: Semiconductor and biotechnology sectors drove 72% of new patent filings
cards.sub: University-industry collaboration produced 850 commercialized research outputs

cards.big: Malaysia trails Singapore at 7th and South Korea at 10th in the index
cards.sub: Gap narrowed but fundamental research output remains significantly lower

cards.big: Innovation ranking tests whether R&D investment trajectory can be sustained long-term
Global Innovation Index rank 32nd, up 5 spots. Rankings improve when R&D spending exceeds 1.5% of GDP consistently.
